text:
On a quiet suburban street lived a wise old owl. Every morning he would watch the children play. They seemed so alive! One autumn afternoon, a gusty wind blew his feathers. An unusually shiny pumpkin came falling, and the owl caught it.

He brought it home and placed it in his garden. The kids thought it was the coolest thing ever. Every child picked pumpkin soup over candy. They carved it spooky faces. That night, many owls watched.

One owl said, "There goes wisdom."

Another disagreed.

"I know! That face watches me!"

A few days later, a little boy wandered over.

"Oh ho ho!" he giggled.

"You do look watchful."

The owl smiled. "Maybe I've learned some things."

That autumn came Christmas. All the little girls wanted a ghost.

That's how the owl got his hat. They called him spooky. But he wore it proudly. Birds would say, "Oh wise owl!"

They liked him. Maybe wisdom wasn't
